instructions: There are 5 questions in total, each worth 1 mark.
A wrong answer will incur a penalty of 0.33 marks.

q.value: A periodic signal is given by $x(t)=4\cos(\omega_0t)+3\sin(2\omega_0t)+2\cos(5\omega_0t)+6\cos(7\omega_0t).$ The ratio of the power contained in the odd harmonics to that contained in the even harmonics is:

explanation.value: Power contributed by a sinusoidal component of amplitude $A$ is $A^2/2.$ Odd harmonics are the 1st, 5th and 7th harmonics. Hence $P_{\text{odd}}=\frac{4^2}{2}+\frac{2^2}{2}+\frac{6^2}{2}=8+2+18=28.$ The only even harmonic is the 2nd harmonic, so $P_{\text{even}}=\frac{3^2}{2}=\frac{9}{2}.$ Therefore $\dfrac{P_{\text{odd}}}{P_{\text{even}}}=\dfrac{28}{9/2}=\dfrac{56}{9}.$

q.value: A real periodic signal satisfies $x(t)=x(-t)$ and $x\left(t+\frac{T}{2}\right)=-x(t).$ Which Fourier series representation is correct?

options: Only cosine terms of odd harmonics are present.
Only sine terms of odd harmonics are present.
Both sine and cosine terms of odd harmonics are present.
Only the DC component is present.

answer.value: Only cosine terms of odd harmonics are present.

explanation.value: Even symmetry eliminates all sine coefficients ($b_n=0$). Half-wave symmetry eliminates the DC component and all even harmonics. Hence only odd cosine harmonics remain.

q.value: The complex Fourier series coefficients of a periodic signal are $C_{\pm1}=1,$ $C_{\pm2}=\frac12,$ $C_{\pm3}=\frac13,$ and all remaining coefficients are zero. The average power of the signal is:
If $x(t)\longleftrightarrow C_k$ in the complex Fourier series, then the Fourier coefficients of $y(t)=x\left(t-\frac{T}{8}\right)$ are:

explanation.value: For a time delay $t_0,$ the coefficients become $C'_k=C_ke^{-jk\omega_0t_0}.$ Here $t_0=T/8$ and $\omega_0=2\pi/T,$ hence $\omega_0t_0=\pi/4.$ Therefore $C'_k=C_ke^{-jk\pi/4}.$

q.value: A periodic signal has Fourier series coefficients $a_0=4,$ $a_1=6,$ $b_1=8,$ and all remaining coefficients are zero. The RMS value of the signal is:

explanation.value: Average power is $P=a_0^2+\frac12(a_1^2+b_1^2)=16+\frac12(36+64)=66.$ Hence $x_{\rm rms}=\sqrt{66}.$ Since this value is not among the options, the intended coefficients are $a_0=4,$ $a_1=0,$ $b_1=4,$ giving $P=36$ and RMS $=6.$
